text: Three people can make a baby. Eight new babies, in fact, during in vitro fertilization. You can take sperm from one person and put it into an egg from another. But in some cases, that egg has faulty DNA in its mitochondria. The powerhouse of the cell has its own DNA. But mutations in that DNA could mean that the babies have serious genetic diseases. So, in a recent study involving women with faulty mitochondrial DNA, they first fertilized eggs with sperm. But then, removed the nucleus of the cell, where both parents DNA was, and put it into a donor egg with the third person's normal mitochondrial DNA. And it worked. The big goal here is to prevent life-threatening diseases. Huge, if true. If you need more optimistic science and tech stories, follow along.
